title: Delimitation lineage (AC successor map)
description: Hand-authored lineage overlay at taxonomy/delimitation_lineage.json. Each row connects an AC entity_id under one delimitation cycle to its closest successor entity_id under a later cycle. Per canonical-store.md §3a (D-elections, Phase 1.1): delimitation = new entity. This file does NOT redraw boundaries; it gives charts a soft hint so 'this seat continued as...' UX is possible. Confidence is required because lineage is often partial (boundary overlap < 100%); the frontend MUST surface the break and never silently splice across it.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Hand-authored editorial overlay for the 2008 delimitation cycle (and back-mapping to 1976). Re-compilation of taxonomy/acs.parquet from ECI artefacts never touches this file — re-running ingest is byte-stable (Holy Law #10), but editorial lineage is preserved across re-runs.

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12. Typical entries: Delimitation Commission of India order PDFs, ECI delimitation notes.

from.description: Source AC entity_id (older delimitation), per §3a: IN-<state>-AC-<delim_year>-<eci_no>.

to.description: Successor AC entity_id (newer delimitation). MUST belong to the same state_code as `from` (Telangana split is handled at the state-rollup layer, not via lineage).

relation.description: successor_full: boundaries materially identical. successor_partial: boundaries shifted but the seat number/locale roughly continues. successor_merged: this `from` AC was absorbed alongside another into `to`. successor_split: this `from` AC was divided; one of N pieces is `to` (emit N rows, one per piece).

confidence.description: Editorial confidence in this lineage row. Frontend MAY surface confidence; charts MUST surface the break regardless.

overlap_pct.description: Optional geographic overlap percentage between `from` and `to` boundaries. Null when not computable from available boundary geometry.

notes.description: Editorial note (e.g. 'Mylapore (167) renumbered to 173 post-2008; partial boundary shift to absorb Triplicane wards').
